zh:
SCP基金会不是一套拥有中央正典的小说系列，而是一座由无数作者共同维护、不断改写的档案迷宫。2007年，一则关于“只在无人注视时移动的雕塑”的短文出现在4chan；它后来成为SCP-173，也成为整座基金会宇宙的第一扇收容门。写作者借用政府报告、实验记录、访谈、删节线与事故附录的口吻，把妖怪、科学幻想、官僚恐怖和网络民俗锁进同一种冷白纸页。基金会的格言是“控制、收容、保护”，但不同故事会把它写成守夜人、秘密帝国、崩坏机器或早已被自身谎言吞没的机构。

这里没有唯一时间线，也没有一位可以裁定真相的作者。SCP-001同时拥有许多互不相容的提案；同一个异常可能在一篇故事中毁灭世界，在另一条“正典”里从未存在。本站因此按“原始条目—作者—当前版本—相关故事”的层级收录，不把同人解释倒灌进原文，也不把网络流行绰号冒充官方对象名。第一卷选取奠定基金会语言与想象力的十二项异常，后续系列可沿同一许可与版本结构继续扩展，而不会破坏既有编号。

SCP Wiki及其文字采用知识共享署名—相同方式共享3.0许可。本站每一条SCP衍生摘要和原创图版都会标出原作者、原页面与许可，并以同一协议开放；任何人都可以再使用，条件是保留署名并继续以相同许可分享。SCP-173曾使用的加藤泉《无题2004》照片不在开放许可内，本卷绝不复制，也不会用相似造型绕开限制。收容始于承认每一份档案真正属于谁。
en:
The SCP Foundation is not a centrally authored series with one canon. It is an archive labyrinth maintained and repeatedly rewritten by thousands of contributors. In 2007, a short post about a sculpture that moved only when unobserved appeared on 4chan; it became SCP-173 and the first containment door into the wider setting. Writers adopted the voice of government reports, experiments, interviews, redactions, and incident addenda, locking monsters, science fiction, bureaucratic horror, and internet folklore into the same cold white file. The Foundation's motto is “Secure, Contain, Protect,” but one story may cast it as a night watch, another as a secret empire, a failing machine, or an institution consumed by its own lies.

There is no single timeline and no author empowered to declare the final truth. SCP-001 has many mutually incompatible proposals; an anomaly may end the world in one tale and never have existed in another canon. Myth Atlas therefore records original article, author, current version, and related stories as separate layers. It does not pour fan interpretation back into the source or promote a popular nickname into an official designation. The opening volume selects twelve anomalies that established the archive's language and imaginative range; later series can extend through the same licensing and version structure without breaking the existing numbers.

The SCP Wiki releases its writing under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0. Every SCP-derived summary and original plate here names the source author, links the article, and carries the same license: reuse is permitted when attribution remains and derivatives are shared alike. The former photograph used for SCP-173—Izumi Kato's Untitled 2004—is not openly licensed. This codex neither copies it nor attempts to evade the restriction through a close imitation. Containment begins by remembering who every file belongs to.
